name: 9q34_subtelomeric_NOTCH1
description: 9q34.3 subtelomeric microdeletion (Kleefstra syndrome) — ~1 Mb terminal deletion encompassing EHMT1 (the dosage-sensitive gene), with breakpoints frequently in subtelomeric SD/9q34 paralog blocks containing NOTCH1/VAV2/ABL1-flanking duplicons. Phenotype: intellectual disability, hypotonia, characteristic facies, autism, cardiac defects.

architecture.internal_structure: Subtelomeric SD cluster (~30-200 kb segments) shared with other acrocentric/subtelomeric regions; unique core contains EHMT1 (primary dosage gene), CACNA1B, ARRDC1, TRAF2, EXD3.
